Explain monads
You said

Explain monads in one paragraph.

A monad is a design pattern that wraps a value and provides a way to chain computations on it without exposing the wrapping. You need three pieces: a type constructor M<T>, a unit, and a bind.

Show a Maybe monad in TypeScript.

Here:

type Maybe<T> = { kind: 'some'; value: T } | { kind: 'none' };
const some = <T>(v: T): Maybe<T> => ({ kind: 'some', value: v });
const none: Maybe<never> = { kind: 'none' };
const bind = <A, B>(m: Maybe<A>, f: (a: A) => Maybe<B>): Maybe<B> =>
  m.kind === 'none' ? m : f(m.value);
